final:
// // This module implements a 31-entry register file with each register having a width of 32 bits. It supports asynchronous write operations controlled by a write disable (wdn) signal, where data is written to a specified register address (waddr) on the falling edge of the clock (clk). Two read addresses (raddr1 and raddr2) allow simultaneous retrieval of data from two registers, outputting the contents to rdata1 and rdata2, respectively.
module top_module(
  input clk,
  input wen,
  input [5:0] waddr,
  input [5:0] raddr1,
  input [5:0] raddr2,
  input [31:0] wdata,
  output [31:0] rdata1,
  output [31:0] rdata2
);reg [31:0] regs [0:30]; // 31 registers, each with 32 bits

  always @(posedge clk) begin
    if (wen) regs[waddr] <= wdata; // write operation
  end

  assign rdata1 = regs[raddr1]; // first read operation
  assign rdata2 = regs[raddr2]; // second read operation

endmodule
